title: Glossary - Canada.ca
content:
Code refers to the status of the person who listed the patent information on the Patent Register. There are three classes:
- "B" - exclusive licensee.
- "C" - has consent of patent owner.
A DIN , or Drug Identification Number, is a unique eight digit number assigned by Health Canada to identify a drug which has been approved for marketing and sale in Canada.
A patent number is a unique seven digit number assigned by the Canadian Intellectual Property Office ( CIPO ) to identify a patent. The number is assigned at the time the patent application is filed. A patent can be viewed by entering the patent number into the CIPO patent database
